summary: Full backend and frontend testing of Japon Bot Dashboard. 11/11 backend pytest cases passed (auth, proxy to Japon Bot API for servidores/estadisticas/tickets/transcript/cerrar-ticket/configurar-servidor, validation, 401 handling). 9/9 frontend flows passed (login wrong+correct, server listing, dashboard 4 stats, tickets page filters/search, config page inputs+validation, logout, session-clear redirect). Modal close-ticket and transcript navigation not exercised via UI because the only available server (PRUEBAS) has 0 tickets - this is data limitation, not a bug.

minor.issue: Sessions stored in a Python set - lost on backend restart and not shared between workers. Acceptable for MVP but flag for production.

action_items: (Optional) Migrate SESSIONS to a persistent store (MongoDB/Redis) so tokens survive backend restart.
(Optional) Add explicit per-field inline validation messages in ConfigPage instead of relying solely on toast - currently a toast appears but no field-level highlighting.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py: SESSIONS is in-memory; all clients will be logged out on backend reload. Consider TTL/JWT.
server.py: japon_request forwards upstream 'detail' which may be a dict; FastAPI will serialize OK but consider normalizing error shape for the frontend.
All endpoints correctly require Bearer token; CORS open to '*' is fine for preview but should be tightened for production.
Frontend testids are consistent (kebab-case) and present across all pages - good for testability.
test_credentials: Admin password: japon2024 (from /app/memory/test_credentials.md). External API key already in backend/.env.
seed_data_creation: None. Used existing real server PRUEBAS (1472440996578201793) from external Japon Bot Railway API.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend is a proxy with header x-api-key to https://japon-bot-production.up.railway.app. Auth is Bearer token stored in an in-memory set. The PRUEBAS server has 0 real tickets so close-ticket modal opening and transcript flow cannot be exercised via UI; backend endpoints for them were verified via pytest (cerrar-ticket schema validation, transcript invalid ID returns upstream error). Frontend testids in kebab-case: view-server-{id}, stat-{total|abiertos|cerrados|reclamados}, goto-tickets-button, goto-config-button, back-dashboard-button, ticket-search-input, ticket-status-filter, close-ticket-{id}, close-ticket-modal-confirm, config-input-{key}, config-save-button, logout-button.
